analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-450

POL-450.title: IMPLEMENT: wire SmartDocs 2.0 bundle-root scaffolding into migrateSmartDocs
POL-450.body:
## Objective

Make `migrateSmartDocs` (`src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts`) scaffold `smartdocs/index.md` and `smartdocs/log.md` whenever they're missing, on every `polaris init`, `polaris init --adopt`, and `polaris adopt` run.

## Context

Analysis [POL-449](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-449>) (scoped from architecture doc `smartdocs/raw/smartdocs-2-0-architecture.md` §7.15, open question 8) confirmed `migrateSmartDocs` as the one function shared by all three entry points (`init.ts`, `adopt-command.ts`, `setup-interview/generate.ts`) and answered the outstanding design questions: scaffold placement, minimal content templates, frontmatter-validation timing, and reconcile/catalog idempotency. Full rationale in `smartdocs/raw/pol-449-bundle-root-scaffolding-analysis.md`.

## Goal

`migrateSmartDocs` calls a new `scaffoldBundleRoot(repoRoot)` step at the end of its existing move loop (existing move/rename/provenance logic untouched). The step creates `smartdocs/index.md` (OKF `okf_version` frontmatter + Governance/Routes template per architecture doc §7.2) and `smartdocs/log.md` (header only, per §3's date-grouped prose convention) only when each file is absent — it never overwrites an existing file. No frontmatter-schema validation is wired in (schema doc doesn't exist yet — §7.5). No `polaris-reconcile`/`polaris-catalog` changes (non-goal — see analysis doc).

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts
* src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.test.ts (new)
* src/cli/init.test.ts
* src/cli/adopt-command.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `scaffoldBundleRoot(repoRoot)` (or equivalent) creates `smartdocs/index.md` and `smartdocs/log.md` when either is missing, called unconditionally at the end of `migrateSmartDocs`
- [ ] Existing files are never overwritten — scaffold is create-if-absent only
- [ ] `smartdocs/index.md` template matches architecture doc §7.2 (frontmatter `okf_version`, `# Governance`, `# Routes` headers)
- [ ] `smartdocs/log.md` template matches architecture doc §3 (reserved filename, date-grouped, newest-first convention header, no seeded entries)
- [ ] Existing move/rename/provenance-recording behavior in `migrateSmartDocs` is unchanged (regression-covered)
- [ ] New unit tests cover: both files missing, both present (no-op), one-of-each partial state
- [ ] `init.test.ts` and `adopt-command.test.ts` each gain at least one assertion that bundle-root files exist after a run
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.test.ts src/cli/init.test.ts src/cli/adopt-command.test.ts

## Ordering

None. Single child, no dependencies.

## Non-goals

* No frontmatter-schema validation wiring (schema doc not yet written — separate future issue)
* No `polaris-reconcile`/`polaris-catalog` re-verification of the bundle-root index (§11 open question 1 stays open)
* No dynamic `# Routes` generation from `.polaris/map/file-routes.json` in the scaffold template (static minimal template only; dynamic route listing is a separate, later concern)
* No `log.md` "why" text sourcing (§11 open question 3 stays open — scaffold only creates the empty header)
* No changes to `seed-instructions.ts` or `adopt-cognition.ts`

POL-451.title: Scaffold smartdocs/index.md and smartdocs/log.md in migrateSmartDocs
POL-451.body:
## Objective

Add bundle-root scaffolding to `migrateSmartDocs` so `smartdocs/index.md` and `smartdocs/log.md` always exist after `polaris init`, `polaris init --adopt`, or `polaris adopt`.

## Context

[POL-450](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-450>) IMPLEMENT parent, single child. Scoped by analysis [POL-449](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-449>) — see `smartdocs/raw/pol-449-bundle-root-scaffolding-analysis.md` for full rationale on template content, placement, and the deferred (non-goal) items.

## Goal

* New function `scaffoldBundleRoot(repoRoot: string): void` in `src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts`.
* Called once, unconditionally, at the end of `migrateSmartDocs` (after the existing move loop, before/alongside `savePlan`/`saveProvenance` — does not touch either).
* Creates `smartdocs/index.md` if absent, using the architecture-doc §7.2 template (frontmatter `okf_version: "0.1"`, `# SmartDocs — Polaris Cognition Bundle` heading, `# Governance` section linking `specs/active/docs-authority-model.md` and `doctrine/active/`, `# Routes` section left as a static placeholder comment — no dynamic file-routes.json generation in this pass).
* Creates `smartdocs/log.md` if absent, using the architecture-doc §3 convention (reserved filename, header only, e.g. `# SmartDocs — Change Log`, no seeded entries — the "why" text source for entries is explicitly out of scope, §11 open question 3).
* Never overwrites either file if it already exists.

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts
* src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.test.ts (new)
* src/cli/init.test.ts
* src/cli/adopt-command.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `scaffoldBundleRoot` creates both files when both are missing
- [ ] `scaffoldBundleRoot` is a no-op (files untouched, byte-identical) when both already exist
- [ ] `scaffoldBundleRoot` creates only the missing file when exactly one exists
- [ ] `migrateSmartDocs` calls `scaffoldBundleRoot` unconditionally, including when there are zero migration steps to process
- [ ] Existing `migrateSmartDocs` move/rename/provenance behavior has zero regressions (existing test coverage, if any, plus new coverage)
- [ ] `init.test.ts` gains an assertion that `smartdocs/index.md` and `smartdocs/log.md` exist after `polaris init`
- [ ] `adopt-command.test.ts` gains an assertion that both files exist after the `consolidate` phase
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.test.ts src/cli/init.test.ts src/cli/adopt-command.test.ts

## Ordering

* None.

## Non-goals

* No frontmatter-schema validation (schema doc not yet written)
* No `polaris-reconcile`/`polaris-catalog` wiring
* No dynamic `# Routes` generation from `.polaris/map/file-routes.json`
* No `log.md` entry-writing logic — scaffold only creates the empty header
* No changes to `seed-instructions.ts` or `adopt-cognition.ts`

POL-449.title: Analyze: wire SmartDocs 2.0 bundle-root scaffolding into migrateSmartDocs (init/adopt)
